name: Checkpoint Restart Workflow
summary: Structure long HPC jobs so they can resume after time limits or preemption.
description: This skill helps users wrap long-running work with checkpoint directories, restart detection, progress state, and Slurm signal handling patterns that make reruns safer and less wasteful.

inputs.name: checkpoint_dir
inputs.description: User-owned directory where restartable state is written.

inputs.name: workload_command
inputs.description: Application command or workflow step that can read and write checkpoint state.

inputs.name: resources
inputs.description: Slurm account, partition, CPU, memory, and wall-time request.

outputs.name: checkpoint_state
outputs.description: Restart marker or application checkpoint files in a durable user-owned directory.

outputs.name: restart_log
outputs.description: Log showing whether the job started fresh or resumed from existing state.

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Run the toy resumable command twice and confirm the second run detects existing progress.
